meta.title: Teresita Castelli · Hand-embroidered miniatures in acrylic
meta.description: Teresita Castelli is an Argentinian artist making delicate hand-embroidered miniatures encased in clear acrylic boxes. Originals and bespoke commissions.

hero.title:
Tiny worlds
stitched by hand,
held in clear acrylic.
hero.lede: I'm Teresita, an Argentinian artist making hand-embroidered miniatures and acrylic-box paintings. Each piece is sewn one thread at a time, then suspended inside a transparent box so you can walk around it.

work.title: Pieces from the studio
work.lede: Each work is one-of-a-kind. Embroidered miniatures: figures, garments, small talismans, mounted on gold leaf and floated inside a handmade clear acrylic box. No two are the same size, and no two are ever repeated.
work.card1Desc: An available original. Embroidered figures on a gentle textile, ready to travel to its new home.
work.card2Desc: A compact world. Available original ready to hang.
work.card3Desc: Each miniature stitched thread by thread. Up close, the scale of the work reveals itself.

process.title:
Slow work,
in small rooms.
process.p1: Each piece begins with a thread and a drawing. The miniatures are embroidered by hand on cotton or silk, sometimes barely two centimeters across. Once finished, they're mounted on gold-leafed panels and sealed inside a custom-cut acrylic box.
process.p2: Nothing is printed. Nothing is batched. A single work takes between two weeks and three months to finish.

statement.title: Teresita Castelli
statement.opening: My work begins with a needle, a thread, and a memory that may or may not have happened.
statement.p1: I embroider miniature worlds — small scenes suspended between what I remember and what I imagine. Each piece is hand-stitched, built slowly, detail by detail, carrying the quiet weight of intimate time.
statement.p2: The acrylic dome that holds each work is not merely a frame. It has physical presence — weight, dimension — yet reads as light and transparent. It draws a real boundary around what lives inside, preserving these worlds intact, sheltered from dust and from forgetting.
statement.p3: I make things that ask to be looked at closely. Things that feel private, almost secret — as if you've stumbled upon something not meant for everyone.

commissions.title: Commissions
commissions.lede: Tell me about the place it will live, the story you want held inside it, and the size. I'll propose a palette and a concept, and we go from there.
commissions.petiteTitle: A piece the size of your palm
commissions.petiteDesc: A single embroidered miniature, floated inside a small acrylic box. For the bedside table, the intimate corner, the gift that says something words can't. Two to four weeks from first conversation to delivery.
commissions.jardinDesc: Nine hand-embroidered miniatures arranged across a gold-leaf field. A world you can spend time in — each figure a character, each placement a choice. Six to ten weeks, because a garden cannot be rushed.
commissions.signatureDesc: The studio's most-requested format. Room for twelve figures across a gold-leaf field — a constellation, a family, a memory held open. Eight to twelve weeks from first sketch to finished work.

contact.title:
Let's make
something small
and unforgettable.
contact.lede: For acquiring a piece, commissioning a new one, or exhibiting the work, write to me directly. I reply to every message personally.

footer.sig: Hecho a mano con hilo, oro y paciencia.
